Also a handy test, if you find that your computer is brand new, is to make sure that you have the right software drivers -- these are like essential tools that allow certain hardware to connect and function properly.  If you have questions about your specific computer, a good place to visit is a computer supported vendor’s website, where they often have helpful info about which pieces of their hardware meet certain minimum requirements for software.  And finally, you can also check to see if that software is supported on your specific operating system.  For example, in Windows, I recommend going to the Windows website to check on that. Human: Can you tell me about the process of 3D printing things? How is it done?

Assistant: Sure, the concept of 3D printing is actually not new, although they’ve made some big recent advances in the technology.  The general idea is that you have a machine with a movable platform, and a laser beam that scans the platform.  The laser beam measures the position of the platform, and based on that information the machine controls a 3D print head that’s mounted above the platform.  The print head has a melted polymer or other print material inside, and it spits out a thin layer of the material onto the platform.  The machine moves the platform downward as it builds the next layer on top of the previous one, until they’ve created the 3D object they want.  It’s a pretty simple process, once you have the machine set up.

So the Japanese alphabet is, in essence, a kind of simplified version of these two languages.  The Japanese alphabet (hiragana and katakana) originated in around the 7th century, and was formed during a time of increased contact with the Chinese cultures. It involves only a handful of ingredients and does not require any preparation prior to cooking. Főzelék also works well in a slow cooker, where you can simmer it for six hours on low heat.

Főzelék

Ingredients:

1 red onion, finely chopped

1 head of cauliflower, broken into florets

2 large carrots, peeled and sliced thinly

1 green pepper, sliced

1 medium white potato, peeled and chopped into large chunks

2 cups of water

1 tsp. paprika

1 tsp. ground coriander

1/2 tsp. red pepper flakes

Salt

Directions:

1.  Add the water, onion, and all of the ingredients to the slow cooker.  Do not add salt at this time.

2.  Allow to cook for 6 hours on low.

3.  Add salt to taste, if desired.

4.  Serve hot.

Human: what is the purpose of a check valve?

Assistant: A check valve is a safety feature that prevents backflow.  In normal operation, the check valve allows fluid to flow in one direction only.  In the event of a reverse flow, the check valve automatically shuts the valve to prevent further flow.  Check valves are used in many places in a home or building, such as plumbing fixtures, shower heads, pipes, and so on.  Without a check valve, you would be at risk of a backflow situation.  If, for example, you have a hot water tank with a water heater underneath, and the heating element goes out, there’s a chance the water temperature could rise and cause hot water to flow back into the tank, potentially causing a fire.  This is where check valves can help you protect your home.

Human: How do you know if a check valve is not working properly?

Assistant: You should regularly check your check valves to make sure they are working properly.  This is especially important if you are installing new check valves, to make sure they’re installed correctly.  Check valves should open only in one direction, and closed in the opposite direction.  If you notice check valve functioning incorrectly, or a leak, you should contact a professional to get it fixed.

You might need to install a check valve into a plumbing fixture yourself, or you might purchase a kit with everything you need to install a check valve. I don’t think it would be scientifically possible or reasonable to “fall in love” with someone at first sight. That would be a kind of love at first sight instant attraction, and the idea behind that is that you can immediately determine deep connections and feelings for another person just by seeing that person for a short time. Without knowing the other person well and getting to know them on a deeper emotional level, it wouldn’t be possible or meaningful for any kind of love to exist. As with many things in life, just because someone may feel strongly attracted to someone else at first sight, there doesn’t necessarily mean that that will lead to any meaningful or long-term relationships. The experience of love at first sight instant attraction is often just a temporary, intense, and bittersweet emotion that doesn’t mean the person will be long-term or happy with the person they are attracted to. To me, real love requires trust and commitment and involves time to develop deeper understanding and caring for someone, so although I can understand the feeling of instant attraction, love at first sight in the truest sense isn’t something I personally believe in.

Assistant: That's an interesting question! It's very likely that your dog is dreaming, as humans and other mammals can, and do, dream. Some animals, including parrots and dolphins, even have the ability to deliberately alter their dreams or even lucid dream by thinking about it while they're dreaming.  Unfortunately, it's not definitively known what dreams are for, or how they actually work, but we do know that dreams are correlated with the generation of new neurons, and sometimes appear to allow our brains to "rewire" in ways that can be helpful for memory and learning.

The different types of behaviors that dogs exhibit during sleep appear to have different functions.  Most of the time, rapid-eye-movement (REM) sleep is characterized by movements, muscle relaxation, and autonomic nervous system activation, and is accompanied by brain waves similar to those seen in a waking state.  Rapid motions like the ones you describe are typical of REM sleep, and can often be detected by their purposeless or repetitive movements.  During light sleep, on the other hand, the dog's limbs are less active, with an alternation between slow movements and periods of inactivity, and technically are still relatively fast-moving compared to deep sleep.  It's also possible that your dog is exhibiting aggression in its dreams, or is experiencing a non-dream-related seizure. Human: What does grading on a curve mean exactly? From what I understand it means giving students better grades than they deserve for some reason.

Assistant: Grading on a curve is also called grading to the curve and grading relative to the curve. It’s a way of assigning final grades to students that is intended to make those grades more equitable. Basically, if you’re grading to the curve it means you grade on a scale that means each student’s grade will be at least some calculated amount higher or lower than they “would” get if you graded based on their relative performance versus the rest of students.  The idea is that having similar raw scores (the scores with no adjustments, which is what grading based on a curve is designed to avoid) is less important than making sure that a particular student doesn’t get absolutely stellar grades, or exceptionally low grades, relative to all her classmates.

Saharan sands float north to Europe, coating cities with dust

The sandstorm, known as a calima in Spain, began covering much of the Iberian Peninsula on Tuesday morning, blanketing cars and buildings in a thick red dust and making it harder to breathe in the stiflingly dry air.

A calima occurs when a burst of dusty, warm wind forms during sandstorms in the Sahara and then crosses over from the African desert. With rain forecast in Madrid on Thursday morning, residents were bracing themselves for a muddy rain.

“There’s not much that can be done at this stage,” said Miguel Serrano, a porter in Madrid, who said he had been busy sweeping dust outside his building Wednesday. “Let’s now see whether the rain helps clean it up or at least makes the air more pleasant.”

While Spain’s skies tended toward the apocalyptic, with blood orange colours reminiscent of areas besieged by wildfires, the effects were more subtle elsewhere.

From the Swiss Alps to Britain, residents of countries far from the Sahara looked out their windows Wednesday and noticed something slightly off. It was not the end times, sky-on-fire hues of Spain, but rather a vague sense that this is not how it usually is.

In London, it was as if the skies had been run through a sepia-toned filter, a slightly unsettling aura that could easily be taken as a harbinger of nothing good. It was the grey-orange colour the sky would be in a movie about a town recovering from nuclear fallout.

Although the phenomenon isn’t new, the European Union’s Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service described this week’s events as “an exceptional Saharan dust episode,” with “very high concentrations of coarse particulate matter.”

Mark Parrington, a senior scientist at the service, said it was not unusual in February and March for wind to kick up sand in the Sahara, sending it on an international voyage as far away as South America. There were traces of Saharan dust in Britain last year as well, he said.

But it is typically not as noticeable as it has been this week, he said. The storm was stronger because of “weather patterns being in the right configuration to bring it directly into Europe,” he said.

In the coming days, the dust is expected to move north through Europe, reaching as far as Denmark, before fading by the weekend, the monitoring service said.

“The current concentrations of particulate matter in the transports are exceptionally high, and some studies predict that climate change will result in even more intense Saharan dust storms in the future,” the service said in a statement. It added that the storms would threaten to worsen air quality, affect the frequency of hurricanes in the Atlantic Ocean and speed the decline of glaciers.

On Wednesday, Spain’s health ministry called the sandstorm an emergency situation and issued a warning to residents to stay indoors and keep doors and windows shut to avoid inhaling particles, particularly people with existing breathing problems. The ministry also warned drivers to show caution because of diminished visibility. Overall, the ministry said people should “reduce all outdoor activity.”

Spain is often on the front line of winds and storms coming from the Sahara and Sahel deserts because it is separated from Morocco only by the narrow Strait of Gibraltar. Still, weather experts said that it was rare for the calima to hit Madrid and other parts of central or northern Spain with such intensity.

Episodes of calima are, however, relatively common in the Canary Islands, a Spanish archipelago off the northwestern coast of Africa. In February 2020, the Canary Islands were hit by their worst sandstorm in 40 years, forcing airports to close at a time of year when the islands receive many tourists from northern Europe seeking a mild winter climate.
